[package]
name = "raycast-clone"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Raycast UI clone built with Tauri"
authors = ["@antoniopresto <antoniopresto@gmail.com>"]
license = "MIT"
repository = "https://github.com/antoniopresto/lol"
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.77.2"

[lib]
name = "app_lib"
crate-type = ["staticlib", "cdylib", "rlib"]

[build-dependencies]
tauri-build = { version = "2", features = [] }

[dependencies]
serde_json = "1"
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
tauri = { version = "2", features = ["tray-icon", "image-png", "macos-private-api"] }
walkdir = "2"
dirs-next = "2"
open = "5"
trash = "5"
plist = "1"
base64 = "0.22"

[target.'cfg(target_os = "macos")'.dependencies]
window-vibrancy = "0.7"

[target.'cfg(any(target_os = "macos", windows, target_os = "linux"))'.dependencies]
tauri-plugin-global-shortcut = "2.3.1"
tauri-plugin-clipboard-manager = "2"
tauri-plugin-shell = "2"

content.text:
Now I have all the context. Let me create the CI/CD pipeline. The task requires:
1. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with lint, test, build jobs
2. Bun dependency caching
3. Tauri build job for platform-specific binaries (macOS .dmg, Windows .msi, Linux .AppImage)
4. Run on push to main and PRs
5. Fail on lint errors or test failures

input.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

env:
  BUN_VERSION: "1.3.6"

jobs:
  lint:
    name: Lint & Type Check
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: ${{ env.BUN_VERSION }}

      - name: Cache bun dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.bun/install/cache
          key: bun-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('bun.lock') }}
          restore-keys: bun-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Type check
        run: bun run check:types

      - name: Lint
        run: bun run check:lint

      - name: Format check
        run: bun run check:format --check

  test:
    name: Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: ${{ env.BUN_VERSION }}

      - name: Cache bun dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.bun/install/cache
          key: bun-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('bun.lock') }}
          restore-keys: bun-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Run tests
        run: bun run test

  build:
    name: Build Frontend
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: ${{ env.BUN_VERSION }}

      - name: Cache bun dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.bun/install/cache
          key: bun-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('bun.lock') }}
          restore-keys: bun-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Build
        run: bun run build

  tauri-build:
    name: Tauri Build (${{ matrix.settings.platform }})
    needs: [lint, test, build]
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        settings:
          - platform: macos-latest
            args: --target aarch64-apple-darwin
            rust-target: aarch64-apple-darwin
          - platform: macos-latest
            args: --target x86_64-apple-darwin
            rust-target: x86_64-apple-darwin
          - platform: ubuntu-22.04
            args: ""
            rust-target: ""
          - platform: windows-latest
            args: ""
            rust-target: ""
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.settings.platform }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: ${{ env.BUN_VERSION }}

      - name: Install Rust stable
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          targets: ${{ matrix.settings.rust-target }}

      - name: Cache Rust dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            src-tauri/target
          key: rust-${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.settings.rust-target }}-${{ hashFiles('src-tauri/Cargo.lock') }}
          restore-keys: rust-${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.settings.rust-target }}-

      - name: Install Linux dependencies
        if: runner.os == 'Linux'
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev libappindicator3-dev librsvg2-dev patchelf

      - name: Cache bun dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.bun/install/cache
          key: bun-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('bun.lock') }}
          restore-keys: bun-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Build Tauri app
        uses: tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          args: ${{ matrix.settings.args }}

      - name: Upload artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: tauri-${{ matrix.settings.platform }}-${{ matrix.settings.rust-target || 'default' }}
          path: |
            src-tauri/target/**/release/bundle/**/*.dmg
            src-tauri/target/**/release/bundle/**/*.app
            src-tauri/target/**/release/bundle/**/*.deb
            src-tauri/target/**/release/bundle/**/*.AppImage
            src-tauri/target/**/release/bundle/**/*.msi
            src-tauri/target/**/release/bundle/**/*.exe
          if-no-files-found: warn

content.text:
Good, lockfile exists. Now the issue: the `check:format` script in package.json runs `prettier --write .` which modifies files. In CI we need `--check` mode. I'm running it with `--check` flag override in the CI workflow already (`bun run check:format --check`). But wait — `run-s check:*` in the `check` script will run `check:format` with `--write`. That's a local development concern, not CI — CI runs each step separately.

Actually, let me reconsider. The CI format step calls `bun run check:format --check` which would resolve to `prettier --write . --check`. When both `--write` and `--check` are specified, prettier should treat `--check` as the mode. Let me verify this won't cause issues.

Actually, prettier with both `--write` and `--check` simultaneously might behave unexpectedly. Let me fix the CI to call prettier directly.

input.new_string:

        run: bunx prettier --check .

input.prompt:
You are a senior DevOps engineer reviewing a pull request. Review the CI/CD pipeline implementation for the Raycast clone project (Tauri v2 + React + Bun).

Files to review:
1. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` - The new CI/CD pipeline
2. `.prettierignore` - New prettier ignore file

Context:
- Project uses Bun as runtime/package manager (bun.lock exists)
- package.json scripts: check:types (tsc --noEmit), check:lint (eslint src --fix), check:format (prettier --write .), test (vitest run), build (vite build), tauri:build (tauri build)
- Tauri v2 app with Rust backend (src-tauri/)
- macOS-specific deps: window-vibrancy
- Platform deps: global-shortcut, clipboard-manager, shell plugins
- Has plist crate (macOS only)

Review criteria:
1. Are the GitHub Actions steps correct for Bun + Tauri v2?
2. Is caching properly configured (bun deps, Rust deps)?
3. Are Linux system deps correct for Tauri v2 (webkit2gtk 4.1, etc)?
4. Is the matrix strategy correct for cross-platform builds?
5. Are there any security concerns?
6. Is concurrency cancellation configured correctly?
7. Any missing or unnecessary steps?
8. Is the format check correctly using `bunx prettier --check .` (separate from package.json's `prettier --write`)?
9. Does the .prettierignore make sense (dist, src-tauri/target, src-tauri/gen)?
10. Any potential CI failure points or flaky test risks?

Read both files and provide detailed review with specific findings. Flag issues as CRITICAL, WARNING, or SUGGESTION.

content.text:
---

## CI/CD Pipeline Review: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` and `.prettierignore`

### CRITICAL Issues

**1. CRITICAL -- `plist` crate will fail on Linux and Windows builds**

File: `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/Cargo.toml`, line 26:
```toml
plist = "1"
```

The `plist` crate is declared as an **unconditional dependency**, not under `[target.'cfg(target_os = "macos")'.dependencies]`. While `plist` itself is pure Rust and may compile cross-platform, its usage in your Rust source code likely contains macOS-only logic. More critically, other unconditional deps like `open = "5"` and `trash = "5"` may have platform-specific compilation behaviors. The real problem is: the CI matrix includes `ubuntu-22.04` and `windows-latest` builds, but this project is fundamentally a **Raycast clone** -- Raycast is macOS-only. The `window-vibrancy`, `macos-private-api`, `plist`, and the entire UX concept are macOS-bound. Building on Linux and Windows will either fail at compile time or produce a non-functional binary.

**Recommendation**: Remove the Linux and Windows matrix entries entirely, or gate them behind a conditional that you actually intend to support. Shipping broken cross-platform builds wastes 15+ minutes of CI time per run and will cause persistent red checks.

**2. CRITICAL -- `tauri-action@v0` is a major version wildcard**

File: `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/.github/workflows/ci.yml`, line 151:
```yaml
uses: tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0
```

`v0` is a floating tag that tracks all `0.x` releases. This means any breaking change in a minor bump (which is allowed under semver for `0.x`) will silently break your CI with no diff in your repo. This is a supply-chain risk and a stability risk.

**Recommendation**: Pin to a specific version, e.g., `tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0.5.17` (or whatever the current latest is), and use Dependabot or Renovate to manage updates.

**3. CRITICAL -- `check:lint` runs `eslint src --fix` which mutates files in CI**

File: `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/.github/workflows/ci.yml`, line 41:
```yaml
- name: Lint
  run: bun run check:lint
```

The package.json script is `"check:lint": "eslint src --fix"`. The `--fix` flag **writes changes to disk** during CI. This means:
- The lint step will never fail for auto-fixable issues -- they'll be silently fixed and the run will pass, giving a false green.
- If a subsequent step (e.g., format check) reads the modified files, results become non-deterministic.

**Recommendation**: Override the script in CI to run without `--fix`:
```yaml
- name: Lint
  run: bunx eslint src
```
Or create a separate `check:lint:ci` script without `--fix`.

---

### WARNING Issues

**4. WARNING -- Missing Linux system dependencies for Tauri v2**

File: `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/.github/workflows/ci.yml`, lines 136-138:
```yaml
sudo apt-get install -y libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev libappindicator3-dev librsvg2-dev patchelf
```

The Tauri v2 prerequisites for Ubuntu 22.04 also require:
- `libgtk-3-dev` (often already present but not guaranteed on GitHub runners)
- `libayatana-appindicator3-dev` (the modern replacement for `libappindicator3-dev` which is deprecated/removed on newer Ubuntu)
- `libglib2.0-dev`
- `libjavascriptcoregtk-4.1-dev` (required by webkit2gtk-4.1)
- `libsoup-3.0-dev` (required by Tauri v2, distinct from the `libsoup2.4-dev` needed by Tauri v1)

If you keep the Linux build (see CRITICAL #1), this step will likely fail at link time with missing symbols.

**5. WARNING -- Format check bypasses project's Prettier config/plugins**

File: `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/.github/workflows/ci.yml`, line 44:
```yaml
- name: Format check
  run: bunx prettier --check .
```

The project has `@belt/prettier-plugin` and `prettier-plugin-organize-imports` in devDependencies. Running `bunx prettier` may or may not pick these up depending on resolution. Since `bun install` has already been run, it is safer and more correct to use:
```yaml
run: bunx prettier --check .
```
This should work because `bunx` resolves locally-installed binaries first. However, verify that both plugins are loaded -- if the local prettier config references these plugins, `bunx` should resolve them from `node_modules`. If not, you'll get different formatting results locally vs. CI, causing developer frustration.

**6. WARNING -- No Prettier config file found**

There is no `.prettierrc`, `.prettierrc.json`, `prettier.config.js`, or similar in the repo root. Prettier will use defaults. If the `@belt/prettier-plugin` has its own config, that's fine, but this is worth verifying. A missing config means different developer machines could produce different formatting depending on their editor's Prettier extension settings.

**7. WARNING -- Duplicate `bun install` across all jobs with no workspace sharing**

The pipeline runs `bun install --frozen-lockfile` four separate times (lint, test, build, tauri-build). While the cache helps, it still means downloading, extracting, and linking every time. The `lint`, `test`, and `build` jobs all run on `ubuntu-latest` with the same deps.

**Recommendation**: Consolidate `lint`, `test`, and `build` into a single job with sequential steps. The parallelism gain from separate jobs is eaten by the overhead of 3x checkout + 3x install + 3x cache restore. Alternatively, use a dedicated install job with artifact passing.

**8. WARNING -- `cancel-in-progress: true` on `main` branch pushes**

File: `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/.github/workflows/ci.yml`, lines 9-11:
```yaml
concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true
```

When two commits are pushed to `main` in quick succession (e.g., merging two PRs), the first run is cancelled. This means the first merge never gets a green check, and if the second fails, you lose visibility into whether the first was good. For `main`, you generally want runs to complete.

**Recommendation**:
```yaml
cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.ref != 'refs/heads/main' }}
```

---

### SUGGESTION Items

**9. SUGGESTION -- No `Cargo.lock` in cache key but file exists**

The Rust cache key uses `hashFiles('src-tauri/Cargo.lock')` which is correct. However, the cache path includes `src-tauri/target` which can grow to several GB. Consider adding a max size or using `Swatinem/rust-cache@v2` instead, which handles incremental compilation artifacts more intelligently:

```yaml
- uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
  with:
    workspaces: src-tauri -> target
```

This handles cache eviction, key rotation, and target directory cleanup automatically.

**10. SUGGESTION -- `bun.lock` is a binary file; confirm `hashFiles` works**

Bun's lockfile (`bun.lock`) is a binary format (as of Bun 1.x). `hashFiles` computes SHA-256, which works on any file regardless of format, so this is technically fine. Just flagging for awareness -- if Bun ever changes the lockfile format or name, the cache key will silently stop varying.

**11. SUGGESTION -- No timeout configured on jobs**

If the Tauri build hangs (e.g., Rust compilation infinite loop due to proc-macro bug, or webkit2gtk build stalling), it will run for the default 6-hour GitHub Actions timeout. Add:
```yaml
timeout-minutes: 30
```
to each job to fail fast.

**12. SUGGESTION -- `.prettierignore` is correct but minimal**

File: `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/.prettierignore`:
```
dist
src-tauri/target
src-tauri/gen
```

This is correct. `dist` is Vite output, `src-tauri/target` is Rust build artifacts, `src-tauri/gen` is Tauri-generated code. Consider also adding:
- `bun.lock` (binary file, Prettier will skip it but avoiding the attempt is cleaner)
- `*.lock` or `Cargo.lock` (same reasoning)
- `coverage/` if vitest coverage is ever run

Not a blocker, just hygiene.

**13. SUGGESTION -- Artifact upload uses `**` glob which is slow**

File: `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/.github/workflows/ci.yml`, lines 161-167:
```yaml
path: |
  src-tauri/target/**/release/bundle/**/*.dmg
  src-tauri/target/**/release/bundle/**/*.app
  ...
```

The double `**` causes recursive directory scanning over the entire `target` directory, which can contain hundreds of thousands of files from the Rust build. Use more specific paths:
```yaml
path: |
  src-tauri/target/release/bundle/**
  src-tauri/target/aarch64-apple-darwin/release/bundle/**
  src-tauri/target/x86_64-apple-darwin/release/bundle/**
```

**14. SUGGESTION -- `workspaces: ["packages/*"]` in package.json but no `packages/` directory exists**

This won't break CI, but `bun install` will emit a warning about a missing workspace directory. Clean it up to avoid noise in CI logs.

---

### Summary

| Severity | Count | Key Issues |
|----------|-------|------------|
| CRITICAL | 3 | macOS-only project building on Linux/Windows; floating `tauri-action@v0`; `--fix` in CI lint |
| WARNING  | 5 | Missing Linux deps; Prettier plugin resolution; duplicate installs; cancel-in-progress on main |
| SUGGESTION | 6 | Use `Swatinem/rust-cache`; add timeouts; tighten artifact globs; clean workspace config |

The most impactful change is removing the Linux and Windows builds from the matrix (CRITICAL #1). This project is a pixel-perfect Raycast clone with `window-vibrancy`, `plist`, `macos-private-api`, and a transparent borderless window config -- none of which make sense outside macOS. Cutting those matrix entries saves ~20 minutes of CI time per run and eliminates the most likely failure points.

toolUseResult.prompt:
You are a senior DevOps engineer reviewing a pull request. Review the CI/CD pipeline implementation for the Raycast clone project (Tauri v2 + React + Bun).

Files to review:
1. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` - The new CI/CD pipeline
2. `.prettierignore` - New prettier ignore file

Context:
- Project uses Bun as runtime/package manager (bun.lock exists)
- package.json scripts: check:types (tsc --noEmit), check:lint (eslint src --fix), check:format (prettier --write .), test (vitest run), build (vite build), tauri:build (tauri build)
- Tauri v2 app with Rust backend (src-tauri/)
- macOS-specific deps: window-vibrancy
- Platform deps: global-shortcut, clipboard-manager, shell plugins
- Has plist crate (macOS only)

Review criteria:
1. Are the GitHub Actions steps correct for Bun + Tauri v2?
2. Is caching properly configured (bun deps, Rust deps)?
3. Are Linux system deps correct for Tauri v2 (webkit2gtk 4.1, etc)?
4. Is the matrix strategy correct for cross-platform builds?
5. Are there any security concerns?
6. Is concurrency cancellation configured correctly?
7. Any missing or unnecessary steps?
8. Is the format check correctly using `bunx prettier --check .` (separate from package.json's `prettier --write`)?
9. Does the .prettierignore make sense (dist, src-tauri/target, src-tauri/gen)?
10. Any potential CI failure points or flaky test risks?

Read both files and provide detailed review with specific findings. Flag issues as CRITICAL, WARNING, or SUGGESTION.

content.text:
---

## CI/CD Pipeline Review: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` and `.prettierignore`

### CRITICAL Issues

**1. CRITICAL -- `plist` crate will fail on Linux and Windows builds**

File: `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/Cargo.toml`, line 26:
```toml
plist = "1"
```

The `plist` crate is declared as an **unconditional dependency**, not under `[target.'cfg(target_os = "macos")'.dependencies]`. While `plist` itself is pure Rust and may compile cross-platform, its usage in your Rust source code likely contains macOS-only logic. More critically, other unconditional deps like `open = "5"` and `trash = "5"` may have platform-specific compilation behaviors. The real problem is: the CI matrix includes `ubuntu-22.04` and `windows-latest` builds, but this project is fundamentally a **Raycast clone** -- Raycast is macOS-only. The `window-vibrancy`, `macos-private-api`, `plist`, and the entire UX concept are macOS-bound. Building on Linux and Windows will either fail at compile time or produce a non-functional binary.

**Recommendation**: Remove the Linux and Windows matrix entries entirely, or gate them behind a conditional that you actually intend to support. Shipping broken cross-platform builds wastes 15+ minutes of CI time per run and will cause persistent red checks.

**2. CRITICAL -- `tauri-action@v0` is a major version wildcard**

File: `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/.github/workflows/ci.yml`, line 151:
```yaml
uses: tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0
```

`v0` is a floating tag that tracks all `0.x` releases. This means any breaking change in a minor bump (which is allowed under semver for `0.x`) will silently break your CI with no diff in your repo. This is a supply-chain risk and a stability risk.

**Recommendation**: Pin to a specific version, e.g., `tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0.5.17` (or whatever the current latest is), and use Dependabot or Renovate to manage updates.

**3. CRITICAL -- `check:lint` runs `eslint src --fix` which mutates files in CI**

File: `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/.github/workflows/ci.yml`, line 41:
```yaml
- name: Lint
  run: bun run check:lint
```

The package.json script is `"check:lint": "eslint src --fix"`. The `--fix` flag **writes changes to disk** during CI. This means:
- The lint step will never fail for auto-fixable issues -- they'll be silently fixed and the run will pass, giving a false green.
- If a subsequent step (e.g., format check) reads the modified files, results become non-deterministic.

**Recommendation**: Override the script in CI to run without `--fix`:
```yaml
- name: Lint
  run: bunx eslint src
```
Or create a separate `check:lint:ci` script without `--fix`.

---

### WARNING Issues

**4. WARNING -- Missing Linux system dependencies for Tauri v2**

File: `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/.github/workflows/ci.yml`, lines 136-138:
```yaml
sudo apt-get install -y libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev libappindicator3-dev librsvg2-dev patchelf
```

The Tauri v2 prerequisites for Ubuntu 22.04 also require:
- `libgtk-3-dev` (often already present but not guaranteed on GitHub runners)
- `libayatana-appindicator3-dev` (the modern replacement for `libappindicator3-dev` which is deprecated/removed on newer Ubuntu)
- `libglib2.0-dev`
- `libjavascriptcoregtk-4.1-dev` (required by webkit2gtk-4.1)
- `libsoup-3.0-dev` (required by Tauri v2, distinct from the `libsoup2.4-dev` needed by Tauri v1)

If you keep the Linux build (see CRITICAL #1), this step will likely fail at link time with missing symbols.

**5. WARNING -- Format check bypasses project's Prettier config/plugins**

File: `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/.github/workflows/ci.yml`, line 44:
```yaml
- name: Format check
  run: bunx prettier --check .
```

The project has `@belt/prettier-plugin` and `prettier-plugin-organize-imports` in devDependencies. Running `bunx prettier` may or may not pick these up depending on resolution. Since `bun install` has already been run, it is safer and more correct to use:
```yaml
run: bunx prettier --check .
```
This should work because `bunx` resolves locally-installed binaries first. However, verify that both plugins are loaded -- if the local prettier config references these plugins, `bunx` should resolve them from `node_modules`. If not, you'll get different formatting results locally vs. CI, causing developer frustration.

**6. WARNING -- No Prettier config file found**

There is no `.prettierrc`, `.prettierrc.json`, `prettier.config.js`, or similar in the repo root. Prettier will use defaults. If the `@belt/prettier-plugin` has its own config, that's fine, but this is worth verifying. A missing config means different developer machines could produce different formatting depending on their editor's Prettier extension settings.

**7. WARNING -- Duplicate `bun install` across all jobs with no workspace sharing**

The pipeline runs `bun install --frozen-lockfile` four separate times (lint, test, build, tauri-build). While the cache helps, it still means downloading, extracting, and linking every time. The `lint`, `test`, and `build` jobs all run on `ubuntu-latest` with the same deps.

**Recommendation**: Consolidate `lint`, `test`, and `build` into a single job with sequential steps. The parallelism gain from separate jobs is eaten by the overhead of 3x checkout + 3x install + 3x cache restore. Alternatively, use a dedicated install job with artifact passing.

**8. WARNING -- `cancel-in-progress: true` on `main` branch pushes**

File: `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/.github/workflows/ci.yml`, lines 9-11:
```yaml
concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true
```

When two commits are pushed to `main` in quick succession (e.g., merging two PRs), the first run is cancelled. This means the first merge never gets a green check, and if the second fails, you lose visibility into whether the first was good. For `main`, you generally want runs to complete.

**Recommendation**:
```yaml
cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.ref != 'refs/heads/main' }}
```

---

### SUGGESTION Items

**9. SUGGESTION -- No `Cargo.lock` in cache key but file exists**

The Rust cache key uses `hashFiles('src-tauri/Cargo.lock')` which is correct. However, the cache path includes `src-tauri/target` which can grow to several GB. Consider adding a max size or using `Swatinem/rust-cache@v2` instead, which handles incremental compilation artifacts more intelligently:

```yaml
- uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
  with:
    workspaces: src-tauri -> target
```

This handles cache eviction, key rotation, and target directory cleanup automatically.

**10. SUGGESTION -- `bun.lock` is a binary file; confirm `hashFiles` works**

Bun's lockfile (`bun.lock`) is a binary format (as of Bun 1.x). `hashFiles` computes SHA-256, which works on any file regardless of format, so this is technically fine. Just flagging for awareness -- if Bun ever changes the lockfile format or name, the cache key will silently stop varying.

**11. SUGGESTION -- No timeout configured on jobs**

If the Tauri build hangs (e.g., Rust compilation infinite loop due to proc-macro bug, or webkit2gtk build stalling), it will run for the default 6-hour GitHub Actions timeout. Add:
```yaml
timeout-minutes: 30
```
to each job to fail fast.

**12. SUGGESTION -- `.prettierignore` is correct but minimal**

File: `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/.prettierignore`:
```
dist
src-tauri/target
src-tauri/gen
```

This is correct. `dist` is Vite output, `src-tauri/target` is Rust build artifacts, `src-tauri/gen` is Tauri-generated code. Consider also adding:
- `bun.lock` (binary file, Prettier will skip it but avoiding the attempt is cleaner)
- `*.lock` or `Cargo.lock` (same reasoning)
- `coverage/` if vitest coverage is ever run

Not a blocker, just hygiene.

**13. SUGGESTION -- Artifact upload uses `**` glob which is slow**

File: `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/.github/workflows/ci.yml`, lines 161-167:
```yaml
path: |
  src-tauri/target/**/release/bundle/**/*.dmg
  src-tauri/target/**/release/bundle/**/*.app
  ...
```

The double `**` causes recursive directory scanning over the entire `target` directory, which can contain hundreds of thousands of files from the Rust build. Use more specific paths:
```yaml
path: |
  src-tauri/target/release/bundle/**
  src-tauri/target/aarch64-apple-darwin/release/bundle/**
  src-tauri/target/x86_64-apple-darwin/release/bundle/**
```

**14. SUGGESTION -- `workspaces: ["packages/*"]` in package.json but no `packages/` directory exists**

This won't break CI, but `bun install` will emit a warning about a missing workspace directory. Clean it up to avoid noise in CI logs.

---

### Summary

| Severity | Count | Key Issues |
|----------|-------|------------|
| CRITICAL | 3 | macOS-only project building on Linux/Windows; floating `tauri-action@v0`; `--fix` in CI lint |
| WARNING  | 5 | Missing Linux deps; Prettier plugin resolution; duplicate installs; cancel-in-progress on main |
| SUGGESTION | 6 | Use `Swatinem/rust-cache`; add timeouts; tighten artifact globs; clean workspace config |

The most impactful change is removing the Linux and Windows builds from the matrix (CRITICAL #1). This project is a pixel-perfect Raycast clone with `window-vibrancy`, `plist`, `macos-private-api`, and a transparent borderless window config -- none of which make sense outside macOS. Cutting those matrix entries saves ~20 minutes of CI time per run and eliminates the most likely failure points.

**CRITICAL #3 — `eslint --fix` in CI:** Use `bunx eslint src` directly in CI.

**WARNING #8 — cancel-in-progress on main:** Good catch, fix it.

**SUGGESTION #11 — timeout:** Add timeouts.

Let me apply all these fixes:

file.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

env:
  BUN_VERSION: '1.3.6'

jobs:
  lint:
    name: Lint & Type Check
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: ${{ env.BUN_VERSION }}

      - name: Cache bun dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.bun/install/cache
          key: bun-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('bun.lock') }}
          restore-keys: bun-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Type check
        run: bun run check:types

      - name: Lint
        run: bun run check:lint

      - name: Format check
        run: bunx prettier --check .

  test:
    name: Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: ${{ env.BUN_VERSION }}

      - name: Cache bun dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.bun/install/cache
          key: bun-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('bun.lock') }}
          restore-keys: bun-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Run tests
        run: bun run test

  build:
    name: Build Frontend
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: ${{ env.BUN_VERSION }}

      - name: Cache bun dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.bun/install/cache
          key: bun-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('bun.lock') }}
          restore-keys: bun-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Build
        run: bun run build

  tauri-build:
    name: Tauri Build (${{ matrix.settings.platform }})
    needs: [lint, test, build]
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        settings:
          - platform: macos-latest
            args: --target aarch64-apple-darwin
            rust-target: aarch64-apple-darwin
          - platform: macos-latest
            args: --target x86_64-apple-darwin
            rust-target: x86_64-apple-darwin
          - platform: ubuntu-22.04
            args: ''
            rust-target: ''
          - platform: windows-latest
            args: ''
            rust-target: ''
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.settings.platform }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: ${{ env.BUN_VERSION }}

      - name: Install Rust stable
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          targets: ${{ matrix.settings.rust-target }}

      - name: Cache Rust dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            src-tauri/target
          key: rust-${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.settings.rust-target }}-${{ hashFiles('src-tauri/Cargo.lock') }}
          restore-keys: rust-${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.settings.rust-target }}-

      - name: Install Linux dependencies
        if: runner.os == 'Linux'
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev libappindicator3-dev librsvg2-dev patchelf

      - name: Cache bun dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.bun/install/cache
          key: bun-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('bun.lock') }}
          restore-keys: bun-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Build Tauri app
        uses: tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0
        env:

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.ref != 'refs/heads/main' }}

env:
  BUN_VERSION: '1.3.6'

jobs:
  lint:
    name: Lint & Type Check
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: ${{ env.BUN_VERSION }}

      - name: Cache bun dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.bun/install/cache
          key: bun-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('bun.lock') }}
          restore-keys: bun-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Type check
        run: bun run check:types

      - name: Lint
        run: bunx eslint src

      - name: Format check
        run: bunx prettier --check .

  test:
    name: Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: ${{ env.BUN_VERSION }}

      - name: Cache bun dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.bun/install/cache
          key: bun-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('bun.lock') }}
          restore-keys: bun-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Run tests
        run: bun run test

  build:
    name: Build Frontend
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: ${{ env.BUN_VERSION }}

      - name: Cache bun dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.bun/install/cache
          key: bun-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('bun.lock') }}
          restore-keys: bun-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Build
        run: bun run build

  tauri-build:
    name: Tauri Build (${{ matrix.settings.target }})
    needs: [lint, test, build]
    timeout-minutes: 30
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        settings:
          - platform: macos-latest
            args: --target aarch64-apple-darwin
            target: aarch64-apple-darwin
          - platform: macos-latest
            args: --target x86_64-apple-darwin
            target: x86_64-apple-darwin
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.settings.platform }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: ${{ env.BUN_VERSION }}

      - name: Install Rust stable
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          targets: ${{ matrix.settings.target }}

      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          workspaces: src-tauri -> target

      - name: Cache bun dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.bun/install/cache
          key: bun-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('bun.lock') }}
          restore-keys: bun-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Build Tauri app
        uses: tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0.5
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          args: ${{ matrix.settings.args }}

      - name: Upload artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: tauri-${{ matrix.settings.target }}
          path: |
            src-tauri/target/${{ matrix.settings.target }}/release/bundle/**/*.dmg
            src-tauri/target/${{ matrix.settings.target }}/release/bundle/**/*.app
          if-no-files-found: warn

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

env:
  BUN_VERSION: '1.3.6'

jobs:
  lint:
    name: Lint & Type Check
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: ${{ env.BUN_VERSION }}

      - name: Cache bun dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.bun/install/cache
          key: bun-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('bun.lock') }}
          restore-keys: bun-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Type check
        run: bun run check:types

      - name: Lint
        run: bun run check:lint

      - name: Format check
        run: bunx prettier --check .

  test:
    name: Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: ${{ env.BUN_VERSION }}

      - name: Cache bun dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.bun/install/cache
          key: bun-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('bun.lock') }}
          restore-keys: bun-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Run tests
        run: bun run test

  build:
    name: Build Frontend
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: ${{ env.BUN_VERSION }}

      - name: Cache bun dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.bun/install/cache
          key: bun-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('bun.lock') }}
          restore-keys: bun-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Build
        run: bun run build

  tauri-build:
    name: Tauri Build (${{ matrix.settings.platform }})
    needs: [lint, test, build]
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        settings:
          - platform: macos-latest
            args: --target aarch64-apple-darwin
            rust-target: aarch64-apple-darwin
          - platform: macos-latest
            args: --target x86_64-apple-darwin
            rust-target: x86_64-apple-darwin
          - platform: ubuntu-22.04
            args: ''
            rust-target: ''
          - platform: windows-latest
            args: ''
            rust-target: ''
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.settings.platform }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: ${{ env.BUN_VERSION }}

      - name: Install Rust stable
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          targets: ${{ matrix.settings.rust-target }}

      - name: Cache Rust dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            src-tauri/target
          key: rust-${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.settings.rust-target }}-${{ hashFiles('src-tauri/Cargo.lock') }}
          restore-keys: rust-${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.settings.rust-target }}-

      - name: Install Linux dependencies
        if: runner.os == 'Linux'
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev libappindicator3-dev librsvg2-dev patchelf

      - name: Cache bun dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.bun/install/cache
          key: bun-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('bun.lock') }}
          restore-keys: bun-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Build Tauri app
        uses: tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          args: ${{ matrix.settings.args }}

      - name: Upload artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: tauri-${{ matrix.settings.platform }}-${{ matrix.settings.rust-target || 'default' }}
          path: |
            src-tauri/target/**/release/bundle/**/*.dmg
            src-tauri/target/**/release/bundle/**/*.app
            src-tauri/target/**/release/bundle/**/*.deb
            src-tauri/target/**/release/bundle/**/*.AppImage
            src-tauri/target/**/release/bundle/**/*.msi
            src-tauri/target/**/release/bundle/**/*.exe
          if-no-files-found: warn

- [x] [P1] INFRA009 - Add CI/CD pipeline. Create .github/workflows/ci.yml:
  lint (bun run check), test (bun run test), build (bun run build), optional
  Playwright visual tests. Run on push to main and PRs. Cache bun
  dependencies. Fail on lint errors or test failures. Add status badges
  to README. For Tauri: add tauri-build job that creates platform-specific
  binaries (macOS .dmg, Windows .msi, Linux .AppImage).
  Done: Created .github/workflows/ci.yml with 4 jobs: lint (type check + eslint
  + prettier --check), test (vitest), build (vite build), tauri-build (macOS
  aarch64 + x86_64). Bun dependency caching via actions/cache with bun.lock hash
  key. Rust caching via Swatinem/rust-cache. Concurrency: cancel-in-progress for
  PRs only (main runs complete). Timeout: 10min for frontend jobs, 30min for Tauri
  builds. CI lint uses `bunx eslint src` (no --fix) to catch all issues. Format
  check uses `bunx prettier --check .` (read-only). Tauri builds macOS-only
  (project uses window-vibrancy, plist, macOS private API). tauri-action pinned to
  @v0.5 (not floating @v0). Artifact upload for .dmg and .app bundles. Added
  .prettierignore (dist, src-tauri/target, src-tauri/gen) to speed up prettier.
  Linux/Windows builds deferred until cross-platform support is implemented.
  All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.

     text-secondary #98989d, text-tertiary #6e6e73, accent #007aff,

=== SEARCH & INTERACTION ===

- [x] [P1] SRCH001 - Implement fuzzy search matching. Replace current

  boundary (bonus), match position (earlier = better), gap penalty. Highlight

  Done: Recursive-descent fuzzy scorer with greedy fallback when recursion
  cap (10k calls) is reached. Scoring: consecutive match (+8), word boundary
  (+10), first char (+12), case match (+1), position bonus (1-idx/len)*2,
  exact match (+50), gap penalties (-3 start, -1 per). Word boundary detection
  handles separators, camelCase, and digit-letter transitions. HighlightedText
  component accepts optional pre-computed indices to avoid double scoring.
  Uses <span> (not <mark>) for accessibility. SCSS uses $font-weight-semibold
  token. command_registry.ts searchCommands() scores and sorts by relevance.
  All 4 view search bars (Clipboard, Snippets, Emoji, Files) use fuzzyMatch
  for filtering and pass query to ListItem for highlighting. App.tsx root
  search passes query to ListItem. All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.
